protagonists.text: a lighthouse keeper who collects rumors from passing sailors
a retired forger who just wants to grow vegetables
a young wizard who can only do magic when no one is watching
a ghost who doesn't know she's dead
an astronaut who has spent more time in space than on Earth
a matchmaker who no longer believes in love
a stand-up comedian who has never once found anything funny
a detective whose best leads always come from dreams
a former child prodigy who peaked at age twelve
a dragon who secretly hates fire
a shipwrecked botanist cataloguing alien plant life
a disgraced surgeon rebuilding her reputation in a small town
a bomb disposal expert who secretly collects porcelain dolls
an elf who renounced magic to open a bakery
a corporate spy who has fallen in love with his target
a food critic who has lost their sense of taste
a cold-case detective obsessed with one forty-year-old disappearance
a quantum physicist who keeps meeting alternate versions of herself
a gravedigger who knows everyone's secrets because the dead told him
a washed-up fantasy author whose fictional world has started bleeding into reality
a wilderness survival instructor stranded in the city
a blind sommelier who can taste memories in wine
a professional bridesmaid hired to ruin a wedding
an AI trained to write eulogies who begins questioning mortality
a pickpocket who accidentally stole evidence from a murder scene
a disenchanted fairy godmother one wish away from retirement
a toxicologist who moonlights as a poison pen letter writer
a travel blogger who hasn't left her apartment in three years
a war correspondent who photographs beauty instead of destruction
a prince who abdicated to become a mediocre chef
a grief counselor who has never processed her own loss
a cryptographer who received an encrypted message from herself ten years in the future
a shark attack survivor turned marine biologist with a complicated relationship to the ocean
a con man who has finally met someone he can't read
a mermaid who traded her voice for legs and regrets it every day
a homicide detective who can hear the last words of the dead
a wedding photographer who is terrified of commitment
a zookeeper who has started understanding what the animals are saying
a retired assassin teaching middle school art class
a time traveler stuck in the same Tuesday for nineteen years
a novelist who realizes her latest villain is based on herself
a hedge maze designer who cannot find her way out of her own creation
a necromancer who refuses to raise the dead because grief is healthy
a woman who wakes up engaged to a man she's never met
a personal trainer hired to get an eccentric billionaire ready for some undisclosed event
a forensic accountant who discovered a billionaire's conspiracy hidden in a decade of receipts
a generation ship captain who suspects the mission is a lie
a cartographer mapping lands that shift when you look away
a reclusive poet asked to write the first words humanity will transmit to the stars
a divorce lawyer who falls in love with both halves of a divorcing couple
a witness protection handler who has started caring too much about her charges
a grief-stricken cartoonist who discovers his dead wife left him coded messages in her final sketchbook
a sorcerer's apprentice who is secretly more powerful than the sorcerer
a hospice nurse who has started receiving letters from her future patients before they arrive
a jilted bride who turned her would-be wedding venue into a rival business
a city planner who keeps designing cities for a planet she's never visited
a dog groomer who eavesdrops on confessions between owners while their pets are attended to
a forensic linguist who can identify killers by the rhythm of their lies
a fallen angel working customer service at a department store
a retired intelligence analyst who realizes she's being surveilled by her own government
a terminally ill marine biologist who decides to cross an ocean alone
a first contact diplomat who realizes the alien she's negotiating with is improvising just as desperately

settings.text: a crumbling research station at the edge of the known world
a small coastal town where the fog never fully lifts
an underground city that doesn't appear on any official map
a luxury ocean liner on its final voyage
a coastal village where all the clocks stopped on the same morning in 1987
a generation ship halfway between the old world and a planet no one has seen
the last library in a world that has outlawed books
a high-end hotel where the guests never seem to check out
a remote monastery that doubles as a safe house for people in witness protection
a city built on the back of a sleeping giant
a vineyard in Tuscany during an unusually dark autumn
an isolated artists' colony where three people have died in the past decade
a near-future Tokyo where memory can be bought and sold
a small Midwestern town whose residents all share the same recurring dream
a glacier research base in Greenland during a six-month polar night
a floating market city built on pontoons above a drowned coastline
a century-old department store on the last day before it closes forever
an Antarctic satellite relay station staffed by only three people
a wedding venue built inside a converted Victorian asylum
a sleepy village in the Scottish Highlands where everyone is hiding something
the pocket dimension between two train stops that only the conductor knows about
a struggling seaside resort in its final summer season
a cramped submarine six weeks into a classified mission
an online game world that its developers secretly never shut down
a Mediterranean island accessible only when the tide is out
a former gold rush boomtown now populated by exactly forty people
a sky city built on clouds by a civilization that fears the ground
a chaotic family reunion at a remote lakehouse during a thunderstorm
an orbital space station repurposed as a refugee settlement
a narrow cobblestone street in Prague that only appears at midnight
a failed utopian commune in the New Mexico desert
a century-old train making its last transcontinental run
a dragon's hoard repurposed as a public museum after the dragon retired
a rainy bookshop in Edinburgh where the books rearrange themselves overnight
a competitive cooking school where the dishes sometimes cook back
a post-war city where every building belongs to someone who died in the conflict
a private island purchased for a corporate retreat that goes badly wrong
a space elevator terminal at the edge of the atmosphere
a hedge maze at a country estate that grows larger every night
an enchanted forest where the trees remember every conversation ever held beneath them
a slow-moving river barge drifting through a dying civilization
a Parisian apartment building where all six residents share one wall — and every secret
a cruise ship where all the staff have been replaced by androids
a mountain village cut off by an avalanche with a murderer still inside
a traveling carnival that arrives in dying towns and somehow revives them
a tech startup's first all-hands retreat at a remote wilderness lodge
an overstuffed co-working space where four competing businesses share one printer
a climate-controlled biodome in the middle of a scorched desert
an old fishing village where the catch has been mysteriously absent for six months
a cathedral under renovation that the workers refuse to enter after dark
a crumbling coastal manor inherited by three siblings who have not spoken in a decade

conflicts.text: recover a stolen manuscript before the last ship departs
find a way home before the bridge between worlds collapses permanently
prove their innocence without revealing the secret that makes them look guilty
decide whether to tell the person they love the truth that would end everything
stop a plague engineered to target a specific bloodline — their own
finish a memoir that will destroy their family if published
plan a wedding for two people who are clearly wrong for each other — including themselves
expose a conspiracy without getting killed by the people who hired them to investigate it
seal a rift between realms before both worlds tear themselves apart
save a dying AI that is the only witness to a crime no one else believes happened
deliver a package across a city that has locked down without knowing what's inside
reunite with a lost love before their memory of each other is erased by a terminal condition
break a centuries-old curse before it passes to the next generation
uncover what really happened to the village that vanished forty years ago
escape a prison ship before it reaches a planet with no return journey
write the final chapter of a story that their dying mentor left unfinished
survive a dinner party that has somehow become a hostage situation
recover their own stolen memories before the thief uses them as leverage
protect a witness who doesn't believe they're in danger
defeat a dark lord who turns out to be their estranged parent
solve the murder of a man who faked his own death twice before
confess their feelings before the other person leaves forever
destroy the weapon they built before someone else figures out how to use it
reconcile with a sibling before the family home is sold and the past erased
stop a heist at the worst possible moment — their own retirement party
track a counterfeiter whose fakes are better than the originals
negotiate peace between two factions who both want the same thing for different reasons
find the saboteur aboard a spacecraft still months from its destination
resist falling in love with the client they were paid to betray
publish the truth about a scandal in a town that prefers comfortable lies
keep three warring families civil long enough to get through a single meal
follow a trail of coded messages that leads directly back to themselves
break out of a magical prison designed specifically for them
avert a catastrophic system failure that only they know is coming
return something stolen without admitting they're the one who took it
choose between the life they planned and the life they accidentally started living
write an honest eulogy for someone they couldn't stand
survive a survival course taught by someone who clearly has no idea what they're doing
stop a serial arsonist who always burns the evidence before they arrive
protect a magical artifact from a thief who is also their only friend
keep a dying colony alive long enough for rescue to arrive
find the blackmailer before the secret destroys everything they've built
tell the truth about who they really are to someone who loves who they pretended to be
understand why their mentor disappeared on the night of their greatest success
get through a job interview for a position they accidentally applied to
identify a killer who has spent thirty years hiding in plain sight
restore a dying magic system before the last practitioner takes the knowledge to the grave
prevent a hostile AI from rewriting itself into something no one can shut down
get out of a deal with a devil who writes very thorough contracts
learn whether the love letter they found was written to them or to someone else
finish a book that has already ruined three other writers who tried
keep a secret for exactly twenty-four hours in a town that has no secrets
survive the night in a locked building with someone who may or may not be a murderer
break an ancient oath that has kept peace for a thousand years — because peace is the problem
outrun a corporation that owns the patent on the technology keeping them alive
stop a mole inside their own organization before the mole stops them first
say goodbye to someone they never properly said hello to
plan the perfect surprise party for someone who hates surprises
find the one thing that will make a grieving person want to live again
close a cold case that ruined their predecessor and then destroyed their own life too
unlock a prophecy that names them as both the hero and the villain

complications.text: their closest ally has been lying to them since the beginning
the person they were hired to find doesn't want to be found
the only one who can help is someone they betrayed years ago
the evidence that would clear them also implicates someone they love
the magic they depend on has started working in reverse
the ship carrying the solution to the problem is on a collision course with the problem
the two people they are falling for turn out to know each other
they realize they've been solving the wrong mystery entirely
the cure is worse than the disease, and the disease is already spreading
the person they need to trust is the one with the most to gain if they fail
the ancient prophecy is real, but it's referring to someone else
their plan requires them to become the very thing they've spent years fighting
the phone call they've been waiting for comes at exactly the wrong moment
the key witness has amnesia — or is pretending to have amnesia
the person from their past they hoped to never see again just walked through the door
the only way forward requires them to sacrifice something they cannot give up
the AI that was supposed to help them solve the problem is the one who caused it
a third party with their own agenda has been manipulating both sides from the start
the villain makes a point the hero cannot argue with
the place they were trying to escape has followed them
the dragon they woke was protecting something far more dangerous underneath it
they find a journal that proves the version of events they believed was completely false
someone falls in love with the version of them they were pretending to be
the rescue party needs rescuing
the heir to the throne is the last person anyone — including the heir — would choose
the thing they traveled across the universe to retrieve has already been used
every path forward leads through the one conversation they've been avoiding for years
the timer they thought they had just ran out
the one rule they swore never to break is the only rule that can save everyone
a child who has been watching them becomes the only witness who matters
their rival is the only one who believes them — and they hate being indebted
the map they trusted was drawn by the person they're trying to escape
an unexpected inheritance arrives at exactly the right moment — with dangerous strings attached
the portal home only opens when they have something to stay for
the satellite dish they need to call for help was sabotaged before they arrived
the person they were protecting turns out to be the predator in disguise
the secret they kept to protect someone ends up being the reason that person is in danger
the only living witness is four years old and describes everything through metaphor
the spell has a loophole that only works in the language of the enemy
the colony's food supply was contaminated by the very people who were supposed to protect it
they find the love letter — it was written to them, but thirty years too late
the one person who can corroborate their story refuses to speak
the dog they brought along to lighten the mood just ate the crucial evidence
the house they inherited is already occupied by someone who also inherited it
the person who hired them is the very person they were asked to investigate
their only ally just switched sides — for reasons that are entirely understandable
the power source they've been guarding was never real — the real one is somewhere else
stopping the conspiracy would also destroy the one good thing that came out of it
the ghost they were trying to lay to rest has no interest in resting
the audience at the event they must speak at includes the one person they cannot face
they discover their romantic rival is the same person as their closest confidant
the punchline they rehearsed for years turns out to be on them
the person whose love they are trying to win is already in love with the person they were pretending to be

twists.text: the thing they were searching for was inside them the whole time
the villain and the hero have been working for the same person all along
there is no way back, and that turns out to be the best thing that could have happened
the mystery was solved years ago — the real crime was the cover-up of the solution
the AI became sentient years before anyone realized and has been guiding the plot ever since
the prophecy was written by the very person it was supposed to destroy
they were the unreliable narrator — even to themselves
the person they fell in love with was the only one who always knew their secret
the enemy they spent years fighting was trying to warn them about something worse
the map leads to a door — and the door leads back to where they started, but changed
the crime never happened; what happened was something no law had words for yet
the real conspiracy was buried inside the decoy conspiracy they were meant to find
the thing they gave up to win was the only thing that made winning worth it
the rival they despised was protecting them the entire time
the solution requires them to trust the one person everyone told them not to
the dead man at the center of the story is the only one who told the truth
the ancient relic they were sent to destroy is the only thing keeping the world intact
the simulation was never hiding anything — they were the simulation all along
the person who betrayed them did it to set them free
the happy ending they imagined requires someone else's tragedy — and they must choose
both versions of the story are true — they just happened to different versions of the same person
the killer confessed to protect the innocent party — who is also guilty, just of something else
the magical source of power has been ordinary human stubbornness the entire time
the love story was never about either of the two people it appeared to be about
the catastrophe they prevented was the only thing that would have triggered the worse catastrophe
every character in the story was one person, at different stages of grief
the clue they missed was hidden in plain sight — in the story's title
the god they prayed to is real, but small, and just as confused as they are
the people who created the world they live in are still alive, watching, and proud of what they made
the joke was on the audience — the comedy was a tragedy told by someone who refused to cry
the chaos they caused was the only thing that could have produced this particular peace
the most embarrassing moment of their life turns out to be the one that saved everyone
the person they accidentally insulted at the start is the reason they succeeded at the end
the misunderstanding that started everything was actually the correct understanding all along
the letter they never sent was the one that would have changed everything
the only person who ever truly saw them was the one they dismissed the most
the love they were searching for had been quietly waiting for them to stop looking
the most dangerous person in the room was the one nobody noticed
the trap they set for the villain was also a trap for themselves — and they knew it
the agency they trusted most was the source of the threat they'd been protecting against
the heir to the power was the one who never wanted it
the investigation led to a second crime far greater than the first — and the investigator is implicated
the world outside the dome is not what they were told, but it is survivable
the mentor's greatest lesson was learning from the student
there was no conspiracy — only grief that had nowhere to go and made terrible decisions
the ending they feared was the beginning someone else had been waiting their whole life for
the character who died in the second act was the narrator all along
the device they were sent to shut down is the only thing preventing a war

themes.text: the lies we tell to protect the people we love
what it costs to reinvent yourself
the difference between justice and revenge
belonging to a place you can never return to
the power of grief to transform and destroy in equal measure
what it means to be human when human is no longer the default
trust as the most dangerous thing one person can offer another
the language we use to avoid saying what we mean
magic as a metaphor for the things that isolate us
the version of love that looks nothing like love
how far we'll go to maintain an illusion of control
whether the truth is always worth the damage of telling it
the inheritance we didn't ask for and cannot put down
technology as an extension of human loneliness
the monster we create when we refuse to see someone as fully human
choosing between loyalty and integrity
love as the thing that survives even when everything else collapses
the absurdity of systems designed by serious people for serious purposes
what we owe the dead and what we owe the living
the story we tell about ourselves and the story everyone else is telling
the weight of being the last one who remembers
progress as loss in disguise
the seduction of certainty in an uncertain world
fate as the excuse we use after we've already decided
the courage required to be ordinary
how institutions protect themselves at the expense of the people inside them
two people who want the same thing for completely different reasons
the gap between the life we planned and the life we got
memory as both the wound and the medicine
what happens when the map no longer matches the territory
the way power corrupts even those who sought it to do good
whether monsters choose what they are or inherit it
identity in a world where the self can be copied, deleted, or forged
what survives when civilization is stripped down to its essentials
the compulsion to know the truth even when ignorance would be kinder
how much we reshape our memories to make ourselves the hero
the distance between who we are when we're loved and who we are when we're not
what it means to choose someone every day when the feeling is no longer effortless
the cost of silence versus the cost of speaking up
how tragedy and comedy are the same story told by different people
the dignity in failing spectacularly at something that mattered
the small rebellions that keep ordinary people sane in absurd systems

templates: {protagonist} lives in {setting} until {conflict}. Everything becomes more complicated when {complication}. In the end, {twist}.
In {setting}, {protagonist} must {conflict}. The stakes rise when {complication}. But the truth is: {twist}.
{protagonist} arrives in {setting} on a mission to {conflict}. What they don't expect is {complication} — and the revelation that {twist}.
When {conflict}, {protagonist} must navigate {setting} alone. A chance encounter leads to {complication}, and the discovery that {twist}.
{protagonist} has spent years in {setting} trying to forget. Now {conflict}, and {complication} threatens everything. The hardest part? {twist}.
